title: GSOC op-committed pings trigger sub-second pulls with ~300ms debounce, polling stays as fallback
content:
## Real-time trigger: GSOC with polling fallback

GSOC is sub-second — direct SOC sync with no Trojan mining on the hot path. Mining a signer targeting a peer's neighborhood costs roughly 10–30 s per (peer, collab) pair, but that cost is paid **once on collab creation/join** and never again.

### Protocol

1. Participant writes a new op → SwarmChannel pushes to their feed.
2. Immediately after, a GSOC ping goes to each *other* participant's signer:

```json
{
  "type": "op-committed",
  "collabId": "drive:<driveId>" | "doc:<driveId>:<docId>",
  "writerAddress": "0xA…",
  "latestIndex": { "global": 42 }
}
```

3. Each recipient's SwarmChannel reacts by pulling that writer's feed on demand, **debounced ~300 ms** so a burst of ops coalesces into a single pull. This prevents a drag-reorder on a large list from triggering dozens of overlapping fetches.
4. The timer-based poll remains as a **fallback** for participants who were offline when the ping was sent. When they come back, the next poll catches up any ops whose pings they missed.

Result: sub-second live updates when everyone is online, eventual consistency otherwise.
description: GSOC op-committed pings trigger on-demand pulls of the writer's feeds with ~300ms debounce; periodic polling remains as a fallback for offline participants.
